summary: The Docker-based reproduction builds Traefik v1.7.34 from source with IP whitelist configured (sourceRange: 10.0.0.0/8, 127.0.0.1/32) and useXForwardedFor=true. A request without spoofing is correctly blocked (403), but a request with X-Forwarded-For: 203.0.113.1, 127.0.0.1 bypasses the whitelist and returns HTTP 200 with the full API configuration, demonstrating the any-match logic vulnerability.
expected_security_impact: Running the reproduction should show that a request without spoofed headers is blocked by the IP whitelist, while a request with a spoofed X-Forwarded-For header containing a whitelisted IP (127.0.0.1) bypasses the whitelist and gains access to the protected API.
actual_observed_result: The request without X-Forwarded-For was blocked (HTTP 403). The request with X-Forwarded-For: 203.0.113.1, 127.0.0.1 returned HTTP 200 with the full provider configuration, confirming the whitelist bypass.
verification_method: The verify script first confirms that requests without the spoofed header are blocked (status 403 or 401), then sends a request with the spoofed X-Forwarded-For header containing 127.0.0.1 and checks that the response is HTTP 200 with the full API configuration. The contrast between the blocked and allowed requests proves the bypass.
evidence_produced_by_run: Request without spoofed X-Forwarded-For was blocked with HTTP 403.
The 200 response contained the full provider configuration, confirming successful bypass of the IP whitelist.
The bypass works because whitelist/ip.go:IsAuthorized checks if ANY IP in the X-Forwarded-For chain matches, not just the rightmost trusted proxy IP.

reproduction.source_acquisition: run.sh clones the target repository on the host, checks out the pinned vulnerable ref v1.7.34, and mounts that checkout into the container at /target-repo.
